families.name: Base Command Manager
families.description: BCM cluster management for AI infrastructure — provisioning, monitoring, configuration, and health management via cmsh and Base View
families.quickRule: Interactive shell? cmsh. Web UI? Base View. Health roles? cmha. Image management? cm-create-image.

tools.name: cmsh
tools.description: Interactive BCM cluster management shell for navigating modes (device, partition, network, category, software image) and applying configuration changes
tools.bestFor: Add/remove nodes, edit categories, update network settings, apply provisioning images, commit configuration changes from CLI

tools.name: base-view
tools.description: Base View browser interface for cluster monitoring, configuration, workload visibility, and health dashboards on the head node
tools.bestFor: Visual cluster overview, workload monitoring, GPU utilization heatmaps, click-through configuration, day-2 operations

tools.name: cmha
tools.description: Manage BCM head-node High Availability — view roles, failover, sync state between active/passive heads
tools.bestFor: Check HA status, force failover for maintenance, validate config sync between head nodes, troubleshoot split-brain

tools.name: cm-create-image
tools.description: Create or update BCM software images used to provision compute nodes (kernel, userland, NVIDIA stack)
tools.bestFor: Build a new compute-node image, update existing image with driver/firmware versions, manage image categories

families.name: Slurm Operations
families.description: Slurm workload manager for training/inference jobs on AI clusters — submit, monitor, account, and administer GPU jobs with GRES and MIG
families.quickRule: Submit batch? sbatch. Interactive? srun. Queue? squeue. Nodes? sinfo. Accounting? sacct/sacctmgr.

tools.name: sbatch
tools.description: Submit a non-interactive batch script to Slurm with GPU GRES, MIG profile, partition, QoS, and time limits
tools.bestFor: Training jobs, multi-node MPI/NCCL runs, scheduled inference batch, reproducible job submissions

tools.name: srun
tools.description: Run a job step interactively or inside a batch script — drives task launch, container integration via pyxis, and resource binding
tools.bestFor: Interactive debug sessions, container execution with --container-image, parallel step launch within an sbatch script

tools.name: squeue
tools.description: Show pending and running jobs, partitions, priority, GRES allocation, and reasons for pending state
tools.bestFor: Check why a job is pending (Reason field), see queue depth per partition, monitor your team's running jobs

tools.name: sinfo
tools.description: Show partition definitions, node states (idle/mix/alloc/drain/down), GRES, and features
tools.bestFor: Find drained nodes, check partition limits, see GPU availability per node, validate scheduler view of fabric

tools.name: scontrol
tools.description: Inspect and modify jobs, nodes, partitions, reservations — drain a node, requeue a job, view full job/node detail
tools.bestFor: Drain node for maintenance, modify a pending job's resources, view scontrol show node for full state, reservations

tools.name: sacct
tools.description: Query Slurm accounting database for completed/failed jobs — elapsed time, MaxRSS, exit code, GPU TRES
tools.bestFor: Audit GPU usage by project, post-mortem failed jobs, chargeback reports, troubleshoot OOM/timeout

tools.name: sacctmgr
tools.description: Manage Slurm accounts, users, associations, QoS, and fair-share — the source of truth for who can submit to which partition
tools.bestFor: Onboard new project/team, set GrpTRES limits per account, configure QoS, set fair-share weights, audit associations
tools.exampleCommand: sacctmgr add account ml-research Description="ML Research" Organization=ai

families.name: Kubernetes Operations
families.description: Kubernetes-based AI workload management with NVIDIA GPU Operator, Network Operator, and DOCA Operator for inference and microservices
families.quickRule: Cluster CLI? kubectl. Charts? helm. GPU support? GPU Operator. Networking? Network Operator. DPU? DOCA Operator.

tools.name: kubectl
tools.description: Primary Kubernetes CLI — view nodes/pods, apply manifests, inspect logs, exec into pods, manage namespaces and RBAC
tools.bestFor: Inspect cluster state, deploy inference services, view GPU operator pods, debug failing inference pods, RBAC management

tools.name: helm
tools.description: Kubernetes package manager — install/upgrade/uninstall charts (GPU Operator, Network Operator, NIM, monitoring stacks)
tools.bestFor: Install NVIDIA GPU Operator, deploy NIM inference, upgrade chart versions, manage release values per environment

tools.name: gpu-operator
tools.description: NVIDIA GPU Operator stack — driver daemonset, container toolkit, device plugin, DCGM exporter, MIG manager
tools.bestFor: Auto-deploy drivers/toolkit/device-plugin per node, validate GPU readiness, configure MIG profiles, expose DCGM metrics

tools.name: network-operator
tools.description: Deploys MOFED, NV peer memory, RDMA shared device plugin, and Multus for high-performance K8s networking on InfiniBand/RoCE
tools.bestFor: Enable GPUDirect RDMA in K8s, expose IB devices to pods, configure secondary networks (Multus), DOCA host integration

families.name: Run:ai Operations
families.description: Run:ai workload orchestration on top of Kubernetes — projects, departments, fractional GPUs, gang scheduling, quotas for AI teams
families.quickRule: User CLI? runai. Submit training? runai submit. Submit inference? runai submit --inference. Quotas? Run:ai UI / kubectl.

tools.name: runai
tools.description: Command-line client for Run:ai — login, switch projects, submit jobs, view workload status, exec into pods
tools.bestFor: Day-to-day data scientist workflow, submit training/interactive/inference workloads, list workloads, get logs

tools.name: runai-project
tools.description: Run:ai project resource — quotas, departments, fairness, over-quota policy, node-pool bindings (via UI or kubectl on Run:ai CRDs)
tools.bestFor: Set per-team GPU quotas, configure department hierarchies, enable fractional/MIG GPU sharing, govern resource fairness

tools.name: runai-inference
tools.description: Submit inference workloads with autoscaling, scale-to-zero, model serving — backed by Knative/KServe under the hood
tools.bestFor: Deploy production inference endpoints, autoscale by traffic, A/B model versions, fractional GPU inference

families.name: Containers & NGC
families.description: Container runtimes and NGC registry for delivering AI workloads — Docker for dev, Enroot/Pyxis for Slurm, NGC for curated images
families.quickRule: Dev? docker. Slurm? pyxis+enroot. Registry? ngc. K8s manifests reference NGC images directly.

tools.name: docker
tools.description: Docker CLI with NVIDIA Container Toolkit for running GPU containers locally — pull from NGC, run, inspect, troubleshoot
tools.bestFor: Single-node dev/test, pulling NGC images, smoke-test inference containers, troubleshoot container startup failures

tools.name: enroot
tools.description: NVIDIA unprivileged container runtime — converts Docker/OCI images to squashfs, runs as user inside Slurm jobs
tools.bestFor: Run containers under Slurm without root, fast image fan-out via shared filesystem, reproducible HPC environments

tools.name: pyxis
tools.description: Slurm SPANK plugin that integrates Enroot with srun/sbatch — adds --container-image and --container-mounts to job launch
tools.bestFor: Run multi-node distributed training in containers via Slurm, mount shared datasets/model dirs, deterministic environment

tools.name: ngc
tools.description: NVIDIA NGC CLI — auth, pull/push containers, manage org/team registry, list NeMo/RAPIDS/NIM assets
tools.bestFor: Authenticate to NGC, pull private team images, push fine-tuned models, list available container versions

families.name: GPU Administration
families.description: Day-2 GPU configuration for AI clusters — MIG partitioning, persistence mode, compute mode, DCGM monitoring
families.quickRule: MIG config? nvidia-smi mig. Telemetry? dcgm-exporter+dcgmi. Health check? dcgmi diag. Quick status? nvidia-smi.

tools.name: nvidia-smi
tools.description: Configure persistence/compute mode, enable/disable MIG, query telemetry, set power/clock limits, view processes
tools.bestFor: Enable MIG mode pre-partition, set persistence for low job-start latency, query telemetry for ops dashboards, manage power caps
tools.permissions: root for config, user for query

tools.name: nvidia-smi mig
tools.description: Create/destroy MIG GPU and Compute Instances, list profiles, view current partitioning per GPU
tools.bestFor: Partition H100/A100 GPUs for multi-tenant inference, create heterogeneous instance sizes, destroy and reconfigure on schedule
tools.exampleCommand: nvidia-smi mig -lgip   # list GPU instance profiles

tools.name: dcgmi
tools.description: Configure DCGM groups, policies, run live telemetry (dcgmi dmon), inspect health and PCIe link quality
tools.bestFor: Live GPU metrics for ops dashboards, configure ECC/page-retirement policies, group GPUs for monitoring scope

tools.name: dcgm-exporter
tools.description: Exports DCGM metrics in Prometheus format — daemonset deployed by GPU Operator for K8s, systemd service for Slurm
tools.bestFor: Wire GPU metrics into Prometheus/Grafana, alert on ECC errors, build chargeback dashboards per project

families.name: Fabric & Storage
families.description: NVLink/NVSwitch fabric (Fabric Manager), InfiniBand health, and AI storage stack troubleshooting — critical for NCCL performance
families.quickRule: NVLink/NVSwitch? nv-fabricmanager. IB port? ibstat. IB perf? ib_write_bw. Filesystem perf? fio / ior.

tools.name: nv-fabricmanager
tools.description: NVIDIA Fabric Manager service that configures NVSwitch on HGX/DGX systems — required for full NVLink mesh between GPUs
tools.bestFor: Verify FM is running before NCCL jobs, troubleshoot NVLink topology issues, restart FM after driver updates

tools.name: ibstat
tools.description: Show InfiniBand HCA port state — Active/Down, link layer, rate (200/400Gb), LID assignment
tools.bestFor: First check when NCCL is slow, validate all IB ports are Active and at expected rate post-install

tools.name: ib_write_bw
tools.description: Point-to-point InfiniBand RDMA write bandwidth benchmark — validate end-to-end fabric performance between two nodes
tools.bestFor: Validate fabric BW matches expected line-rate, isolate slow links/cables, pre-deployment acceptance test
tools.exampleCommand: ib_write_bw -d mlx5_0   # on one node, then ib_write_bw -d mlx5_0 <peer-ip> on other

tools.name: ibdiagnet
tools.description: Mellanox fabric-wide diagnostic — scans subnet for errors, link quality, routing, congestion
tools.bestFor: Post-install fabric audit, find bad cables/ports across the cluster, recurring port-error investigation

tools.name: fio
tools.description: Flexible I/O tester for measuring storage throughput/IOPS/latency on shared filesystems (Lustre, GPFS, BeeGFS, WekaFS)
tools.bestFor: Pre-flight storage validation, baseline performance for AI training datasets, isolate slow-IO complaints
